mission.description: Multi-team platform modernization initiative. Validates MCP reliability under concurrent agent workloads representing realistic enterprise usage patterns.

kluster.description: Infrastructure and tooling modernization track. Owned by platform team, contributors from security, data, and SRE.

tasks.title: Audit legacy service mesh configuration
tasks.description: Review current Istio config across all namespaces. Document deviations from CIS benchmark. Flag any mTLS gaps.

tasks.title: Define zero-trust network policy baseline
tasks.description: Establish default-deny ingress/egress policies. Define allow-list for cross-namespace service communication. Get security sign-off.

tasks.title: Migrate observability stack to OpenTelemetry
tasks.description: Replace proprietary APM agents with OTEL SDK. Validate trace propagation across service boundaries. Update Grafana dashboards.

tasks.title: Implement secrets rotation automation
tasks.description: Wire External Secrets Operator to Infisical. Define rotation schedules per secret class. Test rollback on failed rotation.

tasks.title: Conduct load test on API gateway tier
tasks.description: Simulate 2000 RPS sustained load through Kong. Measure p95/p99 latency. Identify saturation point and document capacity limits.

docs.title: Platform Modernization Runbook
docs.body:
# Platform Modernization Runbook

## Overview
This runbook covers operational procedures for the infrastructure modernization sprint.

## Prerequisites
- kubectl access to `control-plane` and `worker-01` clusters
- Infisical project membership: `platform-ops`
- ArgoCD write access

## Service Mesh Migration
1. Validate current mesh: `istioctl analyze -n production`
2. Apply hardened config: `kubectl apply -f mesh/hardened-baseline.yaml`
3. Verify mTLS: `istioctl x describe pod <pod> -n <ns>`

## Rollback
If mesh config causes connectivity issues:
```bash
kubectl rollout undo deployment/istiod -n istio-system
```

## Contacts
- Platform Lead: platform-lead
- Security Liaison: TBD via #security-review

docs.title: Architecture Decision: Secrets Management
docs.body:
# ADR-014: Centralized Secrets Management via External Secrets Operator

## Status
Proposed

## Context
Currently secrets are managed inconsistently: some in Kubernetes secrets (base64 only), some via manual Infisical lookups, some baked into CI pipelines. This creates audit gaps and rotation blind spots.

## Decision
Adopt External Secrets Operator (ESO) as the single secrets ingestion layer, backed by Infisical as the source of truth.

## Consequences
- **Positive**: Centralized rotation, full audit trail, no secrets in git
- **Negative**: ESO adds a reconciliation loop dependency; outage = no new secret delivery
- **Mitigation**: Cache last-good secret with a 24h TTL as fallback

## Alternatives Considered
- Vault: heavier operationally, not already deployed
- SOPS: no dynamic rotation
- Sealed Secrets: rotation still manual

docs.title: Governance Rules: Platform Team
docs.body:
# Platform Team Governance Rules

## Task Ownership
- Every task must have exactly one `owner`
- `contributors` field required for tasks touching >1 team boundary
- Blocked tasks must have a `dependencies` field citing the blocking task ID

## Definition of Done
A task is complete when:
1. Code reviewed and merged to main
2. Deployed to staging and smoke-tested
3. Runbook updated if operational behavior changed
4. Load test baseline validated if latency-sensitive path changed

## Escalation
- P0 incidents: page `@platform-oncall` immediately
- Architecture changes: require ADR doc in this kluster before merge
- Security-touching changes: require `#security-review` sign-off

## Forbidden Patterns
- No secrets in task descriptions or doc bodies
- No `kubectl exec` in runbooks without explicit audit justification
- No force-push to `main`
